question_text: What causes the Moon's phases as seen from Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: Earth's shadow falling on the Moon

options.label: B
options.text: The different amounts of the Moon's lit side visible from Earth as it orbits

options.label: C
options.text: The Moon moving into Earth's atmosphere

options.label: D
options.text: Changes in the Moon's distance from Earth

explanation: The Moon orbits Earth, and as it does, we see different amounts of its sunlit side. This is why the Moon appears to change shape throughout the month, even though it is always half-lit by the Sun.
